content_en:
It went that Abu Jahl saw Sa’d performing the circumambulation so he asked him who he was. When Sa’d identified himself, Abu Jahl said, ‘Are you circumambulating the Ka’bah safely while you and your tribe at Madeenah have given shelter to Muhammad and his followers?’ Sa’d answered, ‘Yes.’

Then they started quarrelling so Umayyah said to Sa’d, ‘Do not raise your voice in front of Abu Jahl because he is the chief of the people of the valley.’ Sa’d answered, ‘By Allaah, if you prevent me from circumambulating the Ka’bah, | will prevent your caravans from passing by Madeenah on their way to Ash-Sham (Syria). Umayyah kept saying to Sa’d, ‘Do not raise your voice’, and kept on taking hold of him. Sa’d consequently became angry and said, “Enough of you, | heard Muhammad # saying that he would kill you.’ Umayyah said, ‘Me?’ Sa’d answered; ‘Yes’

(Thereafter) Umayyah said to his wife, ‘Have you not known

PP Bo Wor Become Anan what my brother from Yathrib (Madeenah) said?’ She said, ‘What did he say?’ He answered, ‘He said that he heard Muhammad saying that he would kill me.’ She commented, ‘By Allaah, Muhammad never tells lies.’

In another version, Ibn Abi Is-haq narrated that Abdullah bin Abi Nujayh said to him, “Umayyah bin Khalaf was so lazy, venerable, old, fat, and heavy. Once he was sitting among his folk when Ugbah bin Abi Mu’it came to him with brazier, (and said to Umayyah), ‘For you are like women.’ Umayyah said ‘Fie on you and your brazier!” He (the reporter) said to him, ‘Then he got prepared and went out with people.”

In another version, it is said that Abu Jahl kept urging and forcing him to go (with the army) until he said, “Because you have succeeded to convince me (of going), | will buy the best camel in Makkah.”

Contemplate how Ugbah bin Abi Ma’it succeeded in infuriating Umayyah to the extent that he went to buy the best camel to join them at Badr. The method they used to convince him was to label him. Such labelling was considered in Umayyah’s view to be humiliating and lowered his dignity, so he became determined to join them so as to refuse their arguments.

[29] Al-Bukhaaree [30] As-Salihi, Subul Al-Huda War-Rashad.

“Golden Advice Series 03 wes

To remind one of past enmities and grudges: One may want to take his revenge on somebody but he rejects the idea altogether because of his religion. At that time, the hearts are purified, and love and fraternity dominate but the envious and malicious interfere with others to blacken the hearts and sever relations of love and fraternity. They do all this through reminding one of past enmities and grudges that serve a useful purpose for them.

For example, the two tribes of Al-Aws and Al-Khazraj at Madeenah, had wars and a lot of enmity in the pre-Islamic period.'! When Islam appeared, it settled their differences and they became brothers. The Jews at Madeenah felt resentful about that settlement, and tried to provoke them into fighting each other.

It is narrated on the authority of Ibn Humayd that Shas bin Qays was an old man, full of envy and malice towards Muslims, and especially the Prophet’s Companions, for being united and for their brotherhood after their long years of fighting during the pre-Islamic period. He had a meeting with some Muslims and was so surprised by their unity and large number that he said, ‘They are now increasing in numbers greater than us’.

So he ordered a young Jewish man to sit among them and remind them of the battle of Bu’ath in which Al-Aws defeated Al-Khazraj. He recited some of the poems commemorating that battle. The young man did so and the Ansar became so haughty

[31] Aa’ishah '& narrated: “The day of Bu’ath (i.e. Day of fighting between the two tribes of the Ansar, the Aus and Khazraj) was brought about by Allaah for the good of His Prophet 3 so that when Allaah's Messenger #5 reached (Madeenah), the tribes of Madeenah had already divided and their chiefs had been killed and wounded. So Allaah had brought about the battle for the good of His Prophet 3 in order that they (i.e. the Ansar) might embrace Islam.” (Al-Bukhaaree)

“Do Not Become Angry!

and proud that they started to attack one another verbally. After the argument turned into a heated one, they decided to settle it with arms and set the time for the next day at noon.

On the next day, each tribe came with an army and the two armies stood facing each other. Informed of what had happened, the Prophet # and his companions from the Emigrants?) went out to them. He said to them:

“O Muslims! Allaah, Allaah! Do you call to (a thing pertaining to) the pre-Islamic period although | am still amongst you? Are you coming back to be as you used to be (when you were) disbelievers, after Allaah has guided you to Islam, honoured you with it, cut with it your relation with the pre-Islamic period, saved you with it from disbelief, and put affection between you?” **)

The Ansaar realized then that they were acting out of the devil’s temptations and their enemies’ conspiracy, so they put down their arms, held one another and wept. They finally left the battlefield obediently with the Prophet 3s. Thus, Allaah 4% brought the conspiracy of Shas (the enemy of Allaah) to nothing and revealed the following Verses about him and his act:

[32] The Muhajirun — those who migrated (performed Hijrah) from Makkah to Madeenah for the sake of Allaah.

oe Ciirsoe dt ~ 468 Ce Jak ail 5 HEGEL AN hie GE Ge

“Say: ‘O People of the Book! Why reject you the Signs of Allaah, when Allaah is Himself witness to all you do?’ Say: “O you People of the Book! Why obstruct you those who believe, from the Path of Allaah, seeking to make it crooked, While you (yourselves) one witnesses [to Muhammad as a Messenger of Allah and Islaam (Allah’s religion, i.e. to worship none but Him Alone)]? And Allah is not unaware of what you do.” [Aal-Imraan (3):98-99]

And He 4 revealed other Verses about Aws bin Qayzhi, Jabbar bin Sakhr, and those who were with them from their folk who responded to Shas bin Aws’s temptations. These Verses are from:

“seh 134 54 ON ENS SUAS at Cie

“O you who believe! If you listen to a faction among the

People of the Book, they would (indeed) render you

apostates after you have believed!” to “for them is a

dreadful Chastisement.” [Aal-Imraan (3):100-105] Ga]

Being unconscious of the consequences of anger: Finally, being unconscious of the consequences of anger may lead to this flaw.

These consequences may be individual or collective, worldly or concerned with the life to come. Perhaps that is why Allaah calls on man to be well aware of the instructions of the Religion.

“It is not for the Believers to go forth together: if a contingent from every expedition go forth to devote themselves to studies in religion, and admonish the people when they return to them, that thus they (may learn) to guard themselves (against evil).” [At-Tawbah (9): 122]

y 242% [dats oil 3

The Prophet 3 said:

col fp 4ae IS g Glog Ys

“If Allaah wants to do good to a person, He makes him comprehend the Religion...”

The Effects of Anger

Anger has harmful effects and dangerous consequences that may afflict both individuals and the Muslim Ummah. Here are some of these effects and consequences:

Physical harm: Anger springs from the boiling of the blood in the heart which pours forth in the veins as indicated from the

reddening of the face and eyes. The repetition of this process may lead to blood pressure and perhaps arteriosclerosis then paralysis, May Allaah forbid! Thus, we see that anger ends up in physical harm.

Imperfection of one’s religiousness: Anger may lead to backbiting, profanity, robbery, and bloodshed. All these things are sins and imperfection in religiousness.

Being unable to control one’s self: The mind is blurred at moment of anger and thus one is unable to control oneself. So he may do horrible deeds, which he would regret later.!**!

Being exposed to the humiliating state of apologizing: When a man bursts into anger at someone, he may do wrong acts unknowingly and then find himself exposed to the humiliating state of apologizing. The Prophet 3 warned against everything that leads to this state. He said:

[35] While the Messenger of Allaah 38 was sitting with some of his companions, a man reviled Abu Bakr # and insulted him. But Abu Bakr remained silent. He insulted him twice, but Abu Bakr controlled himself. He insulted him thrice and Abu Bakr took revenge on him. Then the Prophet of Allaah 4 got up when Abu Bakr took revenge. Abu Bakr said: ‘Were you angry with me, Prophet of Allaah?’ The Prophet of Allaah 38 replied:

Oe Asie a qe ie ene coy {- 42 29 Sp28l UE HG HK uo gy. Gh ag Bt act vee fy e off ofs Au teed SULA 255 3 GHEY 3ST pb out

“An angel came down from Heaven and he was rejecting what he had said

to you. When you took revenge, a devil came down. | was not going to sit while the devil came down.” (Abu Daawood)

‘Do Not Become Angry! “Avoid everything because of which one should apologize.”"4

Grievous chastisement: The angry person always makes a lot of mistakes and commits a lot of sins, which result in inflicting a stern punishment on him in this life and the life to come. Abdullah Ibn Amr Ibnil Aas 4 asked the Prophet 3s, ‘What might save me from Allaah’s anger?’ He answered:

‘Do not become angry.” *”

On the Muslim Ummah:

Estrangement from the Jama’ah: People enjoy the company of one who is wise and who has his behaviour under control. On the other hand, they detach themselves from the rash and frivolous. Particularly when the one given to anger doubles as a caller to Islaam, he will lose response to his call and this will be a great loss for Islaam as well.
content_id:
Dikisahkan bahwa Abu Jahl melihat Sa'd sedang melakukan tawaf, lalu ia bertanya siapa dirinya. Ketika Sa'd memperkenalkan dirinya, Abu Jahl berkata, 'Apakah kamu melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah dengan aman sementara kamu dan kaummu di Madinah telah memberikan perlindungan kepada Muhammad dan para pengikutnya?' Sa'd menjawab, 'Ya.'

Kemudian mereka mulai bertengkar, lalu Umayyah berkata kepada Sa'd, 'Jangan tinggikan suaramu di hadapan Abu Jahl karena dia adalah pemimpin penduduk lembah ini.' Sa'd menjawab, 'Demi Allah, jika kalian menghalangiku dari tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah, aku akan menghalangi kafilah dagang kalian yang melewati Madinah menuju Syam (Suriah).' Umayyah terus berkata kepada Sa'd, 'Jangan tinggikan suaramu,' dan terus menahannya. Sa'd akhirnya menjadi marah dan berkata, 'Sudah cukup darimu, aku mendengar Muhammad ﷺ mengatakan bahwa beliau akan membunuhmu.' Umayyah berkata, 'Aku?' Sa'd menjawab, 'Ya.'

(Setelah itu) Umayyah berkata kepada istrinya, 'Tidakkah kamu mengetahui

apa yang dikatakan saudaraku dari Yatsrib (Madinah)?' Istrinya berkata, 'Apa yang dikatakannya?' Ia menjawab, 'Dia mengatakan bahwa dia mendengar Muhammad berkata bahwa beliau akan membunuhku.' Istrinya berkomentar, 'Demi Allah, Muhammad tidak pernah berdusta.'

Dalam riwayat lain, Ibn Abi Ishaq meriwayatkan bahwa Abdullah bin Abi Nujayh berkata kepadanya, "Umayyah bin Khalaf adalah orang yang malas, terhormat, tua, gemuk, dan berat. Suatu ketika ia sedang duduk di antara kaumnya ketika Uqbah bin Abi Mu'it datang kepadanya dengan membawa tungku bara api, (dan berkata kepada Umayyah), 'Kamu seperti wanita saja.' Umayyah berkata, 'Celakalah kamu dan tungkumu!' Ia (periwayat) berkata kepadanya, 'Kemudian ia bersiap-siap dan pergi bersama orang-orang.'"

Dalam riwayat lain, disebutkan bahwa Abu Jahl terus mendesak dan memaksanya untuk pergi (bersama pasukan) sampai ia berkata, "Karena kamu telah berhasil meyakinkanku (untuk pergi), aku akan membeli unta terbaik di Makkah."

Renungkanlah bagaimana Uqbah bin Abi Ma'it berhasil membuat Umayyah begitu murka hingga ia pergi membeli unta terbaik untuk bergabung dengan mereka di Badr. Metode yang mereka gunakan untuk meyakinkannya adalah dengan memberinya label. Pelabelan semacam itu dianggap oleh Umayyah sebagai penghinaan dan merendahkan martabatnya, sehingga ia bertekad untuk bergabung dengan mereka demi menolak argumen mereka.

[29] Al-Bukhari [30] As-Salihi, Subul Al-Huda War-Rasyad.

"Seri Nasihat Emas 03

Mengingatkan seseorang akan permusuhan dan dendam lama: Seseorang mungkin ingin membalas dendam kepada orang lain tetapi ia menolak gagasan itu sama sekali karena agamanya. Pada saat itu, hati-hati telah bersih, dan cinta serta persaudaraan mendominasi, tetapi orang-orang yang dengki dan jahat ikut campur untuk menghitamkan hati dan memutuskan tali cinta dan persaudaraan. Mereka melakukan semua ini dengan mengingatkan seseorang akan permusuhan dan dendam lama yang melayani kepentingan mereka.

Sebagai contoh, dua kabilah Al-Aws dan Al-Khazraj di Madinah pernah berperang dan memiliki banyak permusuhan di masa jahiliyah. Ketika Islam datang, Islam mendamaikan perbedaan mereka dan mereka menjadi bersaudara. Orang-orang Yahudi di Madinah merasa tidak senang dengan perdamaian itu, dan mencoba memprovokasi mereka agar saling berperang.

Diriwayatkan berdasarkan otoritas Ibn Humayd bahwa Syas bin Qays adalah seorang tua yang penuh dengki dan kedengkian terhadap kaum muslimin, terutama para Sahabat Nabi, karena persatuan dan persaudaraan mereka setelah bertahun-tahun berperang selama masa jahiliyah. Ia menghadiri sebuah pertemuan dengan beberapa orang muslim dan sangat terkejut dengan persatuan dan jumlah mereka yang banyak sehingga ia berkata, 'Mereka sekarang bertambah jumlahnya lebih banyak dari kita.'

Maka ia menyuruh seorang pemuda Yahudi untuk duduk di antara mereka dan mengingatkan mereka tentang perang Bu'ats di mana Al-Aws mengalahkan Al-Khazraj. Pemuda itu membacakan beberapa syair yang memperingati perang tersebut. Pemuda itu melakukannya dan kaum Anshar menjadi begitu angkuh

[31] Aisyah meriwayatkan: "Hari Bu'ats (yaitu hari peperangan antara dua kabilah Anshar, Aus dan Khazraj) didatangkan oleh Allah untuk kebaikan Nabi-Nya ﷺ sehingga ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba (di Madinah), kabilah-kabilah Madinah sudah terpecah dan para pemimpin mereka telah terbunuh dan terluka. Maka Allah mendatangkan peperangan itu untuk kebaikan Nabi-Nya ﷺ agar mereka (yaitu kaum Anshar) memeluk Islam." (Al-Bukhari)

"Jangan Marah!

dan sombong sehingga mereka mulai saling menyerang secara verbal. Setelah perdebatan berubah menjadi sengit, mereka memutuskan untuk menyelesaikannya dengan senjata dan menetapkan waktunya pada keesokan hari di siang hari.

Keesokan harinya, masing-masing kabilah datang dengan pasukan dan kedua pasukan berdiri saling berhadapan. Setelah diberitahu tentang apa yang terjadi, Nabi ﷺ dan para sahabat beliau dari kalangan Muhajirin pergi menemui mereka. Beliau berkata kepada mereka:

"Wahai kaum muslimin! Allah, Allah! Apakah kalian menyeru kepada (sesuatu yang berkaitan dengan) masa jahiliyah padahal aku masih berada di antara kalian? Apakah kalian kembali menjadi seperti dulu (ketika kalian) masih kafir, setelah Allah membimbing kalian kepada Islam, memuliakan kalian dengannya, memutuskan hubungan kalian dengan masa jahiliyah melaluinya, menyelamatkan kalian dari kekufuran dengannya, dan menanamkan kasih sayang di antara kalian?"

Kaum Anshar kemudian menyadari bahwa mereka bertindak karena godaan setan dan konspirasi musuh-musuh mereka, maka mereka meletakkan senjata mereka, saling berpelukan dan menangis. Akhirnya mereka meninggalkan medan pertempuran dengan patuh bersama Nabi ﷺ. Demikianlah, Allah menggagalkan konspirasi Syas (musuh Allah) dan menurunkan ayat-ayat berikut tentang dirinya dan perbuatannya:

[32] Al-Muhajirin — mereka yang berhijrah dari Makkah ke Madinah demi Allah.

"Katakanlah: 'Wahai Ahli Kitab! Mengapa kamu mengingkari ayat-ayat Allah, padahal Allah Maha Menyaksikan apa yang kamu kerjakan?' Katakanlah: 'Wahai Ahli Kitab! Mengapa kamu menghalangi orang-orang yang beriman dari jalan Allah, kamu menginginkannya menjadi bengkok, padahal kamu menyaksikan (kebenaran Muhammad sebagai Rasul Allah dan Islam sebagai agama Allah, yaitu tidak menyembah selain Dia semata)? Dan Allah tidaklah lengah terhadap apa yang kamu kerjakan.'" [Ali Imran (3):98-99]

Dan Dia menurunkan ayat-ayat lain tentang Aws bin Qayzhi, Jabbar bin Sakhr, dan orang-orang yang bersama mereka dari kaum mereka yang merespons godaan Syas bin Aws. Ayat-ayat tersebut adalah dari:

"Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Jika kamu menaati segolongan dari

Ahli Kitab, niscaya mereka akan mengembalikan kamu menjadi

orang-orang kafir setelah kamu beriman!" hingga "bagi mereka ada

azab yang pedih." [Ali Imran (3):100-105]

Tidak menyadari konsekuensi kemarahan: Terakhir, tidak menyadari konsekuensi kemarahan dapat menyebabkan kelemahan ini.

Konsekuensi ini bisa bersifat individual atau kolektif, duniawi atau berkaitan dengan kehidupan akhirat. Mungkin itulah sebabnya Allah menyerukan kepada manusia untuk memahami dengan baik petunjuk-petunjuk agama.

"Tidaklah sepatutnya orang-orang mukmin pergi semuanya (ke medan perang). Mengapa tidak pergi dari tiap-tiap golongan di antara mereka beberapa orang untuk memperdalam pengetahuan mereka tentang agama dan untuk memberi peringatan kepada kaumnya apabila mereka telah kembali kepadanya, supaya mereka itu dapat menjaga dirinya (dari kejahatan)." [At-Taubah (9): 122]

Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Barangsiapa yang Allah kehendaki kebaikan baginya, maka Allah memahamkannya dalam agama..."

Dampak-Dampak Kemarahan

Kemarahan memiliki dampak yang berbahaya dan konsekuensi yang membahayakan yang dapat menimpa individu maupun umat Islam. Berikut adalah beberapa dampak dan konsekuensinya:

Bahaya fisik: Kemarahan muncul dari mendidihnya darah di dalam hati yang kemudian mengalir ke pembuluh darah sebagaimana ditunjukkan dari memerahnya wajah dan mata. Pengulangan proses ini dapat menyebabkan tekanan darah tinggi dan mungkin pengerasan pembuluh darah kemudian kelumpuhan, semoga Allah melindungi! Dengan demikian, kita melihat bahwa kemarahan berujung pada bahaya fisik.

Ketidaksempurnaan dalam keberagamaan seseorang: Kemarahan dapat menyebabkan ghibah, kata-kata kotor, perampokan, dan pertumpahan darah. Semua hal ini adalah dosa dan kekurangan dalam keberagamaan.

Ketidakmampuan mengendalikan diri sendiri: Akal menjadi kabur pada saat marah sehingga seseorang tidak mampu mengendalikan dirinya. Maka ia mungkin melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan keji yang akan disesalinya kemudian.

Terpapar pada keadaan memalukan untuk meminta maaf: Ketika seseorang meledak dalam kemarahan kepada orang lain, ia mungkin melakukan perbuatan yang salah tanpa sadar dan kemudian mendapati dirinya terpapar pada keadaan memalukan untuk meminta maaf. Nabi ﷺ memperingatkan terhadap segala sesuatu yang mengarah pada keadaan ini. Beliau bersabda:

[35] Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ sedang duduk bersama beberapa sahabatnya, seorang laki-laki mencaci Abu Bakr dan menghinanya. Tetapi Abu Bakr tetap diam. Ia menghinanya dua kali, tetapi Abu Bakr menahan dirinya. Ia menghinanya tiga kali dan Abu Bakr membalas kepadanya. Kemudian Nabi ﷺ berdiri ketika Abu Bakr membalas. Abu Bakr berkata: 'Apakah engkau marah kepadaku, wahai Rasulullah?' Nabi ﷺ menjawab:

"Seorang malaikat turun dari langit dan ia menolak apa yang dikatakan orang itu

kepadamu. Ketika kamu membalas, setan turun. Aku tidak akan duduk selagi setan turun." (Abu Dawud)

'Jangan Marah! "Hindari segala sesuatu yang menyebabkan seseorang harus meminta maaf."

Azab yang berat: Orang yang pemarah selalu melakukan banyak kesalahan dan berbuat banyak dosa, yang mengakibatkan hukuman yang berat baginya di dunia dan akhirat. Abdullah Ibn Amr Ibnil Aas bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ, 'Apa yang bisa menyelamatkanku dari murka Allah?' Beliau menjawab:

'Jangan marah.'

Terhadap Umat Islam:

Keterasingan dari Jama'ah: Orang-orang menikmati kebersamaan dengan seseorang yang bijaksana dan mengendalikan perilakunya. Sebaliknya, mereka menjauhkan diri dari orang yang gegabah dan sembrono. Khususnya ketika orang yang suka marah juga berperan sebagai dai Islam, ia akan kehilangan respons terhadap dakwahnya dan ini akan menjadi kerugian besar bagi Islam pula.
content_ar: It went that Abu Jahl saw Sa’d performing the circumambulation لذا he asked him who he was. عندما Sa’d identified himself, Abu Jahl said, ‘Are you circumambulating the Ka’bah safely بينما you and your tribe at Madeenah have given shelter to محمد ﷺ and his followers?’ Sa’d answered, ‘Yes.’ Then they started quarrelling لذا Umayyah said to Sa’d, ‘Do not raise your voice in front of Abu Jahl لأن he is the chief of the people of the valley.’ Sa’d answered, ‘By Allaah, إذا you prevent me from circumambulating the Ka’bah, | will prevent your caravans from passing by Madeenah on their الطريق to Ash-Sham (Syria). Umayyah kept saying to Sa’d, ‘Do not raise your voice’, and kept on taking hold of him. Sa’d بالتالي became angry and said, “Enough of you, | heard محمد ﷺ # saying that he would kill you.’ Umayyah said, ‘Me?’ Sa’d answered; ‘Yes’ (Thereafter) Umayyah said to his الزوجة, ‘Have you not known PP Bo Wor Become Anan what my الأخ from Yathrib (Madeenah) said?’ Sقال, ‘What did he say?’ رد, ‘قال that he heard محمد ﷺ saying that he would kill me.’ She commented, ‘By Allaah, محمد ﷺ never tells lies.’ In another version, Ibn Abi Is-haq narrated that Abdullah bin Abi Nujayh said to him, “Umayyah bin Khalaf was لذا lazy, venerable, old, fat, and heavy. حين he was sitting among his folk عندما Ugbah bin Abi Mu’it came to him with brazier, (and said to Umayyah), ‘For you are like women.’ Umayyah said ‘Fie on you and your brazier!” He (the reporter) said to him, ‘Then he got prepared and went out with people.” In another version, قيل that Abu Jahl kept urging and forcing him to go (with the army) حتى قال, “لأن you have succeeded to convince me (of going), | will buy the best camel in مكة المكرمة.” Contemplate how Ugbah bin Abi Ma’it succeeded in infuriating Umayyah to the extent that he went to buy the best camel to join them at Badr. The method they used to convince him was to label him. Such labelling was considered in Umayyah’s view to be humiliating and lowered his الكرامة, لذا he became determined to join them لذا as to refuse their arguments. [29] البخاري [30] As-Salihi, Subul Al-Huda War-Rashad. “Golden Advice Series 03 wes To remind one of past enmities and grudges: One may want to take his revenge on somebody but he rejects the idea altogether لأن of his religion. At that time, the القلوب are purified, and المحبة and fraternity dominate but the envious and malicious interfere with others to blacken the القلوب and sever relations of المحبة and fraternity. They do all this through reminding one of past enmities and grudges that serve a useful purpose for them. على سبيل المثال, the two tribes of Al-Aws and Al-Khazraj at Madeenah, had wars and a lot of enmity in the pre-Islamic period.'! عندما الإسلام appeared, it settled their differences and they became brothers. The Jews at Madeenah felt resentful about that settlement, and tried to provoke them into fighting بعضهم بعضاً. روي on the authority of Ibn Humayd that Shas bin Qays was an old man, full of الحسد and malice towards المسلمون, and ولا سيما النبي ﷺ’s الصحابة, for being united and for their brotherhood بعد their long years of fighting خلال the pre-Islamic period. He had a meeting with some المسلمون and was لذا surprised by their unity and large number that قال, ‘They are now increasing in numbers greater than us’. لذا he ordered a young Jewish man to sit among them and remind them of the battle of Bu’ath in which Al-Aws defeated Al-Khazraj. He recited some of the poems commemorating that battle. The young man did لذا and the Ansar became لذا haughty [31] Aa’ishah '& narrated: “The day of Bu’ath (i.e. Day of fighting between the two tribes of the Ansar, the Aus and Khazraj) was brought about by Allaah for the الخير of His النبي 3 لذا that عندما Allaah's Messenger #5 reached (Madeenah), the tribes of Madeenah had already divided and their chiefs had been killed and wounded. لذا Allaah had brought about the battle for the الخير of His النبي 3 in order that they (i.e. the Ansar) might embrace الإسلام.” (البخاري) “Do Not Become Angry! and proud that they started to attack بعضهم بعضاً verbally. بعد the argument turned into a heated one, they decided to settle it with arms and set the time for the next day at noon. On the next day, each tribe came with an army and the two armies stood facing بعضهم بعضاً. Informed of what had happened, النبي ﷺ # and his الصحابة from the Emigrants?) went out to them. قال to them: 4 Qi jeu RAGS a cpDLig Mal esas OF ae; AS ABT 5 BS Sou SARE Lae pal Ke “O المسلمون! Allaah, Allaah! Do you call to (a thing pertaining to) the pre-Islamic period وإن كان | am still amongst you? Are you coming back to be as you used to be (عندما you were) disbelievers, بعد Allaah has guided you to الإسلام, honoured you with it, cut with it your relation with the pre-Islamic period, saved you with it from disbelief, and put affection between you?” **) The Ansaar realized then that they were acting out of the الشيطان’s temptations and their enemies’ conspiracy, لذا they put down their arms, held بعضهم بعضاً and wept. They وأخيراً left the battlefield obediently with النبي ﷺ 3s. وهكذا, Allaah 4% brought the conspiracy of Shas (the enemy of Allaah) to nothing and revealed the following الآيات about him and his act: [32] The Muhajirun — those who migrated (performed Hijrah) from مكة المكرمة to Madeenah for the sake of Allaah. oe Ciirsoe dt ~ 468 Ce Jak ail 5 HEGEL AN hie GE Ge “Say: ‘O People of the الكتاب! Why reject you the Signs of Allaah, عندما Allaah is Himself witness to all you do?’ Say: “O you People of the الكتاب! Why obstruct you those who believe, from the الطريق of Allaah, seeking to make it crooked, بينما you (yourselves) one witnesses [to محمد ﷺ as a Messenger of الله and Islaam (الله’s religion, i.e. للعبادة none but Him Alone)]? And الله is not unaware of what you do.” [Aal-Imraan (3):98-99] And He 4 revealed other الآيات about Aws bin Qayzhi, Jabbar bin Sakhr, and those who were with them from their folk who responded to Shas bin Aws’s temptations. These الآيات are from: “seh 134 54 ON ENS SUAS at Cie SH Us Gall GB es Late of TH o8 Ye A ASHp : Jw ds aS Re Oe “O you who believe! إذا you listen to a faction among the People of the الكتاب, they would (indeed) render you apostates بعد you have believed!” to “for them is a dreadful Chastisement.” [Aal-Imraan (3):100-105] Ga] Being unconscious of the consequences of الغضب: وأخيراً, being unconscious of the consequences of الغضب may lead to this flaw. These consequences may be individual or collective, worldly or concerned with the الحياة to come. Perhaps that is why Allaah calls on man to be well aware of the instructions of the Religion. Here are some of these effects and consequences: Physical harm: الغضب springs from the boiling of the blood in the القلب which pours forth in the veins as indicated from the reddening of the face and eyes. The repetition of this process may lead to blood pressure and perhaps arteriosclerosis then paralysis, May Allaah forbid! وهكذا, we see that الغضب ends up in physical harm. Imperfection of one’s religiousness: الغضب may lead to backbiting, profanity, robbery, and bloodshed. All these things are الذنوب and imperfection in religiousness. Being unable to control one’s self: The العقل is blurred at moment of الغضب and وهكذا one is unable to control oneself. لذا he may do horrible deeds, which he would regret later.!**! Being exposed to the humiliating state of apologizing: عندما a man bursts into الغضب at someone, he may do الخطأ acts unknowingly and then find himself exposed to the humiliating state of apologizing. النبي ﷺ 3 warned against everything that leads to this state. قال: [35] بينما الرسول ﷺ of Allaah 38 was sitting with some of his الصحابة, a man reviled أبو بكر # and insulted him. But أبو بكر remained silent. He insulted him twice, but أبو بكر controlled himself. He insulted him thrice and أبو بكر took revenge on him. Then النبي ﷺ of Allaah 4 got up عندما أبو بكر took revenge. أبو بكر said: ‘Were you angry with me, النبي of Allaah?’ النبي ﷺ of Allaah 38 replied: Oe Asie a qe ie ene coy {- 42 29 Sp28l UE HG HK uo gy. Gh ag Bt act vee fy e off ofs Au teed SULA 255 3 GHEY 3ST pb out “An angel came down from Heaven and he was rejecting what he had said to you. عندما you took revenge, a الشيطان came down. | was not going to sit بينما the الشيطان came down.” (Abu Daawood) ‘Do Not Become Angry! “Avoid everything لأن of which ينبغي للمرء apologize.”"4 Grievous chastisement: The angry person always makes a lot of mistakes and commits a lot of الذنوب, which result in inflicting a stern العقاب on him in this الحياة and the الحياة to come. Abdullah Ibn Amr Ibnil Aas 4 asked النبي ﷺ 3s, ‘What might save me from Allaah’s الغضب?’ رد: ‘Do not become angry.” *” On the مسلم الأمة: Estrangement from the Jama’ah: People enjoy the company of one who is wise and who has his السلوك under control. من ناحية أخرى, they detach themselves from the rash and frivolous. Particularly عندما the one given to الغضب doubles as a caller to Islaam, he will lose response to his call and this will be a great loss for Islaam as well.
